title: WHITESPACE
text: WHITESPACE Operation activated by the SAX parser when it encounters character data that it considers to be ignorable whitespace. Explain this code operation WHITESPACE params string Chars: in endparams Implement your code here end; WHITESPACE operation Copy Parameters Chars —whitepsace characters such as spaces, tabs, carriage returns, and line feeds that the parser considers ignorable. Description If there is no DTD for the XML document being processed, this operation is never called. Instead, whitespace is reported together with other character data by activating the CHARACTERS operation. If there is a DTD, the parser consideres any whitespace it encounters ignorable and activates the WHITESPACE operation, passing it the whitespace characters encountered. For example, the following XML contains a carriage-return and some leading spaces between <aaa> and <bbb> . <aaa> <bbb>text</bbb> </aaa> Copy The parameter passed to WHITESPACE will contain the carriage-return and these spaces. Since ignorable whitespace is usually ignored, this operation is normally not implemented. Related Links CHARACTERS Adds the supplied text inside an element. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
